clusters.title: Meta and YouTube Found Liable in Landmark Addiction Trial
clusters.summary: A jury has ruled that Meta, the parent company of Instagram, and YouTube are liable in a significant trial concerning social media addiction. This landmark decision marks a pivotal moment in the ongoing debate over the responsibilities of social media platforms in relation to user mental health and addiction issues. The outcome could have far-reaching implications for how these companies operate and are regulated in the future.

clusters.title: Trump's Signature to Feature on US Currency for the First Time
clusters.summary: The U.S. Treasury Department has announced that former President Donald Trump's signature will appear on U.S. paper currency, marking a significant departure from a 165-year tradition of excluding the signatures of living individuals. This decision has sparked discussions about its implications for the symbolism of currency and the role of political figures in such representations. The move reflects ongoing debates regarding Trump's legacy and influence in American politics.

items.title: Trump's signature to appear on US currency, Treasury announces - USA Today
items.description: Trump's signature to appear on US currency, Treasury announces  USA Today

items.title: Trump’s Signature to Being Added to All New US Paper Currency
items.description: The Treasury Department announced on Thursday that President Donald Trump’s signature is being added to all new U.S. paper currency. It will be printed on the bills in honor of the United States’ 250th anniversary. It’s the first time a sitting president’s signature will go on paper currency.

clusters.title: Senate Approves Funding Deal for DHS, Excluding ICE
clusters.summary: The Senate has approved a funding deal that secures financial support for most of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), including the Transportation Security Administration (TSA), while notably excluding funding for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). This agreement aims to prevent a government shutdown and ensure continued operations within key security agencies. The decision reflects ongoing debates over immigration enforcement and resource allocation within the federal government.

The move aims to alleviate concerns over the financial impact on TSA personnel as the shutdown persists.

clusters.title: Savannah Guthrie to Return to 'Today' Show Amid Mother's Search
clusters.summary: Savannah Guthrie is set to return as co-anchor of NBC's 'Today' show on April 6, following a two-month absence due to her mother Nancy's disappearance on February 1. The search for Nancy Guthrie continues in Arizona, drawing public attention to both the case and Savannah's personal circumstances. Her return marks a significant moment for the show as it navigates this ongoing situation.

clusters.title: IOC Excludes Transgender Women from Female Olympic Events
clusters.summary: The International Olympic Committee has implemented a new eligibility policy that bans transgender women athletes from competing in female events. This decision has sparked mixed reactions, with some viewing it as a necessary step for fairness in women's sports, while others, including officials in France, criticize it as a regression in inclusivity. The policy marks a significant change in the IOC's approach to gender and competition.

This incident has drawn significant international attention, with testimonies from affected families highlighting the tragedy's impact. The call for accountability underscores the ongoing concerns regarding civilian safety in conflict zones.

clusters.title: Man Sentenced for Assault Witnessed by Barron Trump via Video Call
clusters.summary: Matvei Rumiantsev, a 23-year-old Russian man, has been sentenced to four years in prison for assaulting a woman in London. The incident occurred in January 2025 and was reported to UK police by Barron Trump, who witnessed the attack during a video call from the United States. This case highlights the involvement of high-profile individuals in everyday incidents and raises questions about safety and accountability.

items.title: Man jailed for assaulting woman in London attack witnessed by Barron Trump
items.description:
Matvei Rumiantsev, who became jealous of woman’s friendship with US president’s son, jailed for four years

A Russian man has been jailed for four years for assaulting a woman in an attack in London that was witnessed on a video call by Donald Trump’s youngest son, Barron.

Matvei Rumiantsev, 23, attacked the woman when he became jealous of her friendship with Trump, 19, after she met him through social media, a court heard.

Continue reading...

items.title: Man jailed for four years after Barron Trump reported attack to UK police
items.description: The US president’s youngest son contacted officers when he was in the US to say his friend had been attacked in January 2025.
